sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see the g/G pattern clearly:
- g nodes: 4 separate nodes at positions around row 15-18, col 31-34
  - g: row=[15,15], col=[32,33] - top part
  - g: row=[16,17], col=[31,31] - left part
  - g: row=[16,17], col=[34,34] - right part
  - g: row=[18,18], col=[32,33] - bottom part
- G node: row=[14,19], col=[42,47] - this is the border around the cross at (16, 44)
The g pattern is at col [31,34], row [15,18]. This is to the LEFT of the cross at (16, 44). The cross is at col [43,46].
Wait, the g pattern is at col [31,34], which is to the left of the cross at col [37,40] (the other cross). So the g pattern is appearing at the position of the OTHER cross!
Actually, looking more carefully:
- Cross 1: row=[15,18], col=[37,40] (left cross in top grid)
- Cross 2: row=[15,18], col=[43,46] (right cross in top grid)
- g pattern: around row 15-18, col 31-34
The g pattern is at col [31,34], which is to the LEFT of cross 1 at col [37,40]. This is the position where the cross would move if it went LEFT instead of RIGHT.
Wait, but I clicked the cross at (16, 44), which is cross 2 (right cross). The g pattern appeared at col [31,34], which is to the left of cross 1. This doesn't make sense.
Let me reconsider. The g pattern forms a cross shape:
- Top: row 15, col 32-33
- Left: row 16-17, col 31
- Right: row 16-17, col 34
- Bottom: row 18, col 32-33
This is a cross shape centered at approximately row 16-17, col 32-33. This is to the LEFT of cross 1 at col [37,40].
But I clicked cross 2 at col [43,46]. Why would the g pattern appear at col [31,34]?
Wait, maybe the g pattern is appearing at the position where the cross WOULD move if it could. In level 1, crosses moved to the RIGHT. In level 2, maybe crosses need to move to the LEFT?
Actually, looking at the image, I can see a gray cross pattern at the left side of the top grid. This is the g pattern! It's a gray cross shape at approximately row 15-18, col 31-34.
So when I click a cross, a gray (g) version of the cross appears at a different position. This might be a preview of where the cross will move, or it might be a "ghost" cross that needs to be activated.
The G border is at row=[14,19], col=[42,47], which surrounds the cross I clicked at col [43,46]. This is the same as level 1 where clicking a cross produces a G border around it.
So the mechanic seems to be:
1. Click a cross to select it (G border appears)
2. A gray (g) ghost cross appears at the destination
3. Something needs to happen to confirm the move
But what confirms the move? In level 1, the cross just moved automatically. In level 2, maybe I need to use ACTION7 or click the ghost cross.
Let me try clicking the ghost cross at (16, 33) to see if that confirms the move.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model (updated):
- Clicking cross at (16, 44) produces:
  - G border around clicked cross (row 14-19, col 42-47)
  - g ghost cross at row 15-18, col 31-34 (to the LEFT)
- The g ghost cross is a preview of where the cross will move
- Need to confirm the move - maybe by clicking the ghost cross
Plan: Click the ghost cross at (16, 33) to confirm the move

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
from collections import Counter

# Click the ghost cross at (16, 33) to confirm the move
print("=== Click ghost cross at (16, 33) ===")
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 16, 'col': 33}])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
N_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
g_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'g']
G_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'G']
print(f"After click: N={len(N_nodes)}, g={len(g_nodes)}, G={len(G_nodes)}")

for n in N_nodes:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    print(f"  N: row=[{min(rows)},{max(rows)}], col=[{min(cols)},{max(cols)}]")

print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
